channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters march to stadium ahead of World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Pro-Palestinian protesters lit flares and waved flags as they marched to the stadium ahead of the World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel on Saturday. Public broadcaster NRK said around 1,000 protesters arrived at the Ullevaal Stadion in Oslo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ivory Coast arrests 237 protesters amid rising tensions before presidential election
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — At least 237 people were arrested Saturday in Ivory Coast during a protest against what activists called the country's authoritarian drift, according to a statement by the Minister of the Interior and Security on national television.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jared Kushner reenters as key player in Middle East peace push
item.description: Jared Kushner has reentered the political spotlight as a top envoy for the Trump White House in efforts to secure a peace deal in the Middle East, highlighting both his influence in the region and his growing business portfolio there.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: A Sudanese paramilitary attack kills at least 53 people in Darfur, an aid group says
item.description: A doctors' group says that Sudanese paramilitary forces have attacked a shelter in a besieged Darfur city, killing at least 53 people.
item.dc:creator: By Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hillary Clinton, Condoleezza Rice commend Trump over Israel-Hamas deal
item.description: Former Secretaries of State Hillary Clinton and Condoleezza Rice on Friday commended President Trump for successfully facilitating the first stages of a ceasefire deal with Israel and Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: France’s newly re-appointed Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu acknowledged that he might be able to stay in his job long given the country's deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump envoy Witkoff visits IDF base in northern Gaza as peace deal advances, thousands return home
item.description: White House Special Envoy Steve Witkoff visited an IDF base in northern Gaza on Saturday as thousands of Palestinians were seen returning to their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins and US troops land in Israel as Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians are returning to neighborhoods in Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is in its second day.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana, Sam Metz, Sarah El Deeb And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Lee Keath, Julia Frankel And Jalal Bwaital, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to visit Egypt and Israel early next week amid ceasefire deal in Middle East
item.description: President Trump said Friday he plans to visit Egypt and Israel during a one-day trip to the Middle East early next week to mark the implementation of a peace agreement that will see a pause in fighting in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts as 'really doing a lot' to resolve global crises and conflicts
item.description: President Donald Trump's efforts to negotiate peace deals, including the Israel-Hamas truce, received praise from Russian President Vladimir Putin at a summit in Tajikistan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces 100 percent tariff on China in response to rare earth controls
item.description: President Trump announced Friday he will raise tariffs on China in response to a move from Beijing to tighten its control over certain critical minerals and rare earth elements. Trump announced on Truth Social that he would impose a tariff of 100 percent on Chinese goods beginning Nov. 1 or sooner.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Dutch far-right politician Wilders fears he may have been a target of Belgian attack plotters
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Dutch far-right politician Geert Wilders said Friday that he’s suspending campaigning for national elections later this month following reports that he was a possible target of a suspected plot in Belgium to kill politicians using a drone carrying explosives.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin praises Trump's peace efforts despite failing to win the Nobel prize
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Friday praised U.S. President Donald Trump's peace efforts, despite not winning a Nobel, and voiced hope for securing an agreement to extend a pivotal nuclear arms pact with Washington for another year.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Taiwan's president pledges to build air defense system in face of China threat
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan will accelerate the building of a “Taiwan Shield” or “T-Dome” air defense system in the face of the military threat from China, its leader said Friday. President Lai Ching-te also pledged to raise defense spending to more than 3% of GDP and to reach 5% by 2030.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: Philippine seismologists say that a new offshore earthquake with a preliminary 6.8 magnitude hit the same southern region struck earlier by an earthquake with a magnitude of 7.4.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Peace celebrations in Gaza marred by daunting rebuilding phase to come
item.description: A freelance photographer based in Gaza details his hope for life in the war-torn city now that a ceasefire deal has been reached.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Exclusive: Israeli ambassador says no peace in Gaza unless Hamas hands over all 48 hostages, disarms
item.description: Israel's Ambassador Yechiel Leiter warns the Gaza war will continue unless Hamas returns all hostages and completely disarms under the new peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump peace deal triggers 72-hour countdown for Hamas to release 48 hostages from Gaza
item.description: Israeli security cabinet will convene Thursday to approve the Hamas hostage deal, starting a 96-hour countdown for the release of 48 hostages held for over two years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas accepts Trump peace plan ending 2 years of war in Gaza, returning hostages
item.description: Hamas agrees to President Donald Trump's Gaza peace plan that calls for release of all 48 hostages and complete Israeli withdrawal in exchange for Hamas disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe takes on 'hybrid war' as Germany allows police to shoot down drones
item.description: EU President Ursula von der Leyen warns Russia's 'hybrid warfare' targeting Europe through airspace violations and cyber attacks needs to be directly acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza peace talks appear to gain momentum on the war's anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Peace talks between Israel and Hamas resumed at an Egyptian resort city on Tuesday, the two-year anniversary of the militant group's surprise attack on Israel that started the war that has killed tens of thousands of Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump thanks group representing families of hostages for Nobel Peace Prize appeal
item.description: President Donald Trump thanked the Hostages and Missing Families Forum for its appeal to the Nobel committee to award the president the Nobel Peace Prize.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine says it struck Russian ammunition plant, oil terminal and weapons depot
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Long-range Ukrainian drones and missiles hit a major Russian ammunition plant, a key oil terminal and an important weapons depot behind the front line, Ukraine's president and military said Monday, as Kyiv cranked up pressure on Moscow’s military logistics.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Negotiators meet in Egypt seeking Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Talks are split into phases: first, securing the release of hostages, then tackling Hamas' demilitarization.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, WGN 9
